interactions.summary: Jiang opens the first class by framing the course as training in connected ideas, then uses Kant to define reality as mediated imagination and models the course as prediction-testing across past, present, and future before beginning a banking thought experiment.
A student challenges the claim that economics teaches a zero-money answer in the bank example.

speaker_notes.note: SPEAKER_01 is the instructor and is almost certainly Jiang.
SPEAKER_00 is a student voice briefly objecting from the class.

claims.claim: Kant matters to the course because he shows that humans do not access objective reality directly; perception transforms the unknowable thing-in-itself into appearances structured for human processing.
The course is not meant to dictate conclusions but to augment imagination so students can build and test better models of reality.
The 'secret history' project begins from the premise that school history is a power-implanted system and that understanding manipulation is the route to liberation.
The opening money example asks students to compare the intuitive zero-money answer with what the banking system actually does.

signature_moments.moment: Reality is not a thing handed to us; it is an act of imagination that must be trained.
signature_moments.source_phrase: reality is what we imagine it to be
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This gives the whole lecture its method: power rules by controlling the imagination, so liberation requires remaking perception.

signature_moments.moment: School history is introduced as implanted software, not neutral memory.
signature_moments.source_phrase: a system implanted into your brains by powerful people
signature_moments.why_it_matters: The lecture's later attacks on money, individualism, and the nation-state all grow from this claim about manufactured reality.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's ASR-rendered term for Kant's unknowable things-in-themselves, contrasted with phenomena.
The course project of using present models and predictions to reinterpret the past behind official school history.

chronology_notes.note: This is the first Secret History class, dated 2025-08-21, and it lays out the course-level framework rather than a later retrospective position.

uncertainty_notes.note: The transcript renders Kant's noumena/phenomena terminology phonetically; public prose should normalize cautiously or note the ASR issue.
